scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within chemistry and laboratory computation scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Largest-fragment heuristic is the standard cheminformatics approach for de-salting; co-crystal caveat documented.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles cleanly; RDKit integration is correct and functional. However, --keep-largest still uses type=bool (line 268) instead of the documented lambda x: x.lower() == 'true' fix. Path traversal rejection documented but not confirmed in script.

recommendations.title: --keep-largest still uses type=bool in script — documented fix not applied
recommendations.problem: scripts/main.py line 268 still uses type=bool for --keep-largest. argparse type=bool treats any non-empty string as True, so '--keep-largest false' silently passes True. The documented lambda fix has not been applied.
recommendations.root_cause: Notes documents the lambda x: x.lower() == 'true' fix but the script was never updated.
recommendations.fix: In scripts/main.py line 268, change type=bool to type=lambda x: x.lower() == 'true'. This is a one-character change that fixes the silent bool parsing bug.

recommendations.title: No explicit handling for zwitterionic compounds
recommendations.problem: Zwitterionic compounds (e.g., amino acids with both positive and negative charges) may be incorrectly de-salted by the largest-fragment heuristic.
recommendations.root_cause: Salt ion identification rules focus on inorganic and organic acid salts; zwitterions are not mentioned.
recommendations.fix: Add to Notes: 'Zwitterionic compounds (e.g., amino acids) may require manual review — the largest-fragment heuristic may incorrectly strip a charged moiety. Verify results for compounds with both positive and negative charges.'
